name: caldav-blade-mcp
title: Calendar (CalDAV)
description: CalDAV calendar operations — protocol-level access to any CalDAV server
tagline: Universal calendar access over the CalDAV protocol
author: groupthink-dev

highlights.text: Events — list, search, create, update, delete across all calendars
Today & week views — token-efficient daily and weekly snapshots
Free/busy — check availability across calendar sets
Batch operations — fetch multiple date ranges in a single call
Write-gated — reads always work, writes require explicit opt-in
Any provider — iCloud, Fastmail, Google, Nextcloud, Radicale

readme:
### What it does

CalDAV Blade gives your AI agent direct protocol-level access to any CalDAV calendar server — iCloud, Fastmail, Google, Nextcloud, Radicale, or any standards-compliant provider. 13 tools cover the full calendar lifecycle: listing calendars, querying events by date range, searching by keyword, creating and updating events, checking free/busy availability, and moving events between calendars.

### Token efficiency

Calendar APIs return verbose iCalendar data full of timezone definitions, recurrence rules, and organiser metadata. Each tool includes a concise formatter that extracts only the fields your agent needs — title, time, location, description, and attendees. The `cal_today` and `cal_week` views provide pre-formatted daily and weekly snapshots in a single call, and `cal_events_batch` fetches multiple date ranges without round-trips.

### Safety model

All 13 tools follow a read-free, write-gated pattern:

- **Reads** work immediately — list calendars, query events, check availability
- **Writes** require `CALDAV_WRITE_ENABLED=true` — create, update, delete, move events

This means your agent can explore your calendar freely but cannot make changes unless you explicitly enable write access.

### Provider compatibility

| Provider | CalDAV URL | Auth |
|----------|-----------|------|
| iCloud | `https://caldav.icloud.com` | App-specific password |
| Fastmail | `https://caldav.fastmail.com/dav/calendars` | API token |
| Google | `https://apidata.googleusercontent.com/caldav/v2` | OAuth or app password |
| Nextcloud | `https://your-server/remote.php/dav` | Username + password |

tools.name: cal_info
tools.description: Health check — list providers, connection status, calendar count, write gate status.

tools.name: cal_calendars
tools.description: List all calendars across all providers — returns name, UID, and provider for each.

tools.name: cal_events
tools.description: Get events from a single calendar in a date range. Compact one-line-per-event output.

tools.name: cal_events_batch
tools.description: Fetch events from multiple calendars in one call, grouped by calendar — preferred for digests.

tools.name: cal_event
tools.description: Get a single event by UID with full details including attendees, recurrence, and description.

tools.name: cal_search
tools.description: Search events by text, attendee email, or location. Optional calendar and date scope.

tools.name: cal_today
tools.description: Today's events across all calendars, grouped by calendar. Compact output.

tools.name: cal_week
tools.description: This week's events across all calendars, grouped by calendar. Compact output.

tools.name: cal_freebusy
tools.description: Free/busy availability across one or more calendars in a date range.

tools.name: cal_create
tools.description: Create a new event. Write — gated by CALDAV_WRITE_ENABLED.

tools.name: cal_update
tools.description: Update an existing event by UID. Write — gated by CALDAV_WRITE_ENABLED.

tools.name: cal_delete
tools.description: Delete an event by UID. Destructive — gated by CALDAV_WRITE_ENABLED.

tools.name: cal_move
tools.description: Move an event between calendars. Write — gated by CALDAV_WRITE_ENABLED.

env.name: CALDAV_PASSWORD
env.description: CalDAV account password or app-specific password

setup.help_text: Provide your CalDAV server URL, username, and password. For iCloud: use appleid.apple.com → Sign-In and Security → App-Specific Passwords. For Fastmail: use an API token as the password. For Google Calendar: use a Google app password.
setup.blurb: CalDAV calendar operations — protocol-level access to any CalDAV server

scenarios.label: Morning schedule briefing
scenarios.body: Fetch today's events with cal_today, check for double-bookings using cal_freebusy, and summarise the day's commitments with travel time and prep notes.

scenarios.label: Reschedule a conflicting meeting
scenarios.body: Search for the conflicting event, check free/busy slots across attendees' calendars, then update the event to the next available window.

scenarios.label: Weekly calendar audit
scenarios.body: Pull the full week view with cal_week, identify gaps and over-scheduled days, and flag events that overlap with known focus-time blocks.
